paper_title: Pollination and Gene Flow in Alfalfa

methods.description: This method describes the setup and execution of pollination trials using different bee species to study gene flow in alfalfa plants. The trials were conducted in a controlled greenhouse environment to observe the transfer of glyphosate-resistant (GR) genes from donor to recipient plants.

steps.description: Set up cages in a greenhouse with specific dimensions and materials.
steps.searchable_summary: construct experimental cages in greenhouse using PVC and mosquito netting

steps.description: Place GR alfalfa plants in the small cage and conventional alfalfa plants in the large cage.
steps.searchable_summary: arrange GR and conventional alfalfa plants in designated cages

steps.description: Train bees to visit alfalfa plants in the small cage using blooming conventional alfalfa plants.
steps.searchable_summary: train bees to forage on alfalfa plants

steps.description: Release bees into the training cage and allow mating for leafcutting bees.
steps.searchable_summary: release and allow mating of bees in training cage

steps.description: Set up trials by placing flowering GR alfalfa plants in the small cage and conventional flowering alfalfa plants in the large cage.
steps.searchable_summary: initiate trials with specific plant placements

methods.method_name: Model Selection for Analyzing Gene Flow
methods.description: This method involves selecting and fitting generalized linear mixed models (GLMM) to analyze the probability of obtaining GR seeds in alfalfa pods based on bee foraging behavior.

steps.description: Fit GLMM with a binomial error distribution and logit link function to examine the relationship between the logit of the probability of getting GR seeds and predictor variables.
steps.searchable_summary: fit GLMM with binomial distribution and logit link

steps.description: Evaluate additional models using natural log and square root transformations of predictor variables.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate models with log and sqrt transformations

steps.description: Fit mixed effect models with random intercept and slope for each bee species and predictor variable.
steps.searchable_summary: fit mixed effect models with random effects

steps.description: Compare models using Akaike Information Criterion (AIC) to select the best fit.
Illustrate the back transformed logit and observed data points for the best models.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize back transformed logit and data points
